Class InstrumentStatisticsSummary

java.lang.Object
grafioschtrader.reports.InstrumentStatisticsSummary
public class InstrumentStatisticsSummary extends Object
Comprehensive statistical analysis service for individual financial instruments, providing detailed performance metrics and risk analysis in both the instrument's native currency and the tenant's main currency. Calculates annualized returns, volatility measures, and distribution statistics across multiple time horizons.

This service performs sophisticated financial analysis including yield calculations, annualized performance over various periods (1, 3, 5, 10 years), and comprehensive statistical measures such as standard deviation, minimum, and maximum returns. All calculations consider dividend reinvestment and are performed with proper currency conversion when the instrument's currency differs from the tenant's main currency.

Key Analysis Features:

  • Annualized performance calculations for standard investment horizons
  • Multi-period statistical analysis (daily, monthly, annual)
  • Risk metrics including volatility and return distribution
  • Automatic currency conversion with missing pair creation
  • Dividend-adjusted return calculations
  • Performance comparison in multiple currencies

Currency Handling:

The service intelligently handles multi-currency scenarios by automatically detecting when currency conversion is needed, locating appropriate currency pairs, and creating missing pairs as necessary. This ensures accurate performance analysis regardless of the instrument's trading currency.

Statistical Rigor:

Uses Apache Commons Math for robust statistical calculations, ensuring professional-grade accuracy in all mathematical operations including standard deviation, annualized returns, and compound growth calculations.

    The tenant's main currency (only set when a security needed currency resolution).

    Direction of the currency conversion for the resolved pair: true when close prices must be divided by the currency-pair close, false when they must be multiplied.

    Whether the instrument needs no currency conversion, i.e. its currency already equals the tenant main currency or the instrument is a currency pair.

    • InstrumentStatisticsSummary

  • Method Details

    • getSecurityCurrency

      public Securitycurrency<?> getSecurityCurrency()
      The resolved security or currency pair after prepareSecurityCurrencypairs(Integer) ran.
      Returns:
      the analysed instrument

    • isSecurityTenantSameCurrency

      public boolean isSecurityTenantSameCurrency()
      Whether the instrument needs no currency conversion, i.e. its currency already equals the tenant main currency or the instrument is a currency pair.
      Returns:
      true when no conversion to the main currency is required or possible

    • getCurrencypairs

      public List<Currencypair> getCurrencypairs()
      Currency pairs required to convert the instrument currency into the tenant main currency, or null when no conversion is needed.
      Returns:
      the conversion currency pairs, or null

    • getCurrencyOfSecurity

      public String getCurrencyOfSecurity()
      ISO currency code of the analysed security, or null for a currency pair.
      Returns:
      the instrument currency

    • getTenantCurrency

      public String getTenantCurrency()
      The tenant's main currency (only set when a security needed currency resolution).
      Returns:
      the tenant main currency, or null

    • isCurrencyDivide

      public boolean isCurrencyDivide()
      Direction of the currency conversion for the resolved pair: true when close prices must be divided by the currency-pair close, false when they must be multiplied.
      Returns:
      true to divide, false to multiply

    • prepareSecurityCurrencypairs

      @Transactional public void prepareSecurityCurrencypairs(Integer idSecuritycurrency)
      Prepares the security and currency pair configuration for statistical analysis. Determines whether the security requires currency conversion and sets up the necessary currency pairs for accurate multi-currency calculations.

      This method handles both direct securities and currency pairs, automatically detecting the analysis requirements and configuring the appropriate currency conversion mechanisms. For securities in different currencies than the tenant's main currency, it locates or creates the necessary currency pairs.

      Parameters:
      idSecuritycurrency - the unique identifier of the security or currency pair to analyze

    • getAnnualisedSecurityPerformance

      public AnnualisedPerformance getAnnualisedSecurityPerformance()
      Calculates comprehensive annualized performance metrics for the security across multiple investment horizons. Provides both year-by-year performance and annualized compound returns for standard investment periods (1, 3, 5, 10 years).

      The analysis includes dividend reinvestment calculations and provides results in both the security's native currency and the tenant's main currency. The method handles currency conversion automatically and ensures accurate performance measurement regardless of currency differences.

      Performance calculations use compound annual growth rate (CAGR) methodology for annualized returns and include dividend distributions for total return analysis. The results enable comprehensive performance evaluation and comparison across different investment horizons.

      Returns:
      comprehensive annualized performance data including year-by-year returns and multi-year compound annual growth rates in both currencies

    • getStandardDeviation

      public StatisticsSummary getStandardDeviation(org.springframework.jdbc.core.JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate, LocalDate dateFrom, LocalDate dateTo, boolean adjustCurrency) throws IllegalAccessException, InvocationTargetException, NoSuchMethodException
      Calculates comprehensive statistical measures including volatility, return distribution, and risk metrics across multiple time periods. Provides detailed statistical analysis for risk assessment and performance evaluation in both currencies.

      This method computes professional-grade statistical measures using Apache Commons Math for maximum accuracy and reliability. The analysis includes:

      • Daily volatility and return distribution statistics
      • Monthly volatility for medium-term risk assessment
      • Annual volatility for long-term risk evaluation
      • Minimum and maximum return values for range analysis

      All calculations properly handle currency conversion and provide results in both the instrument's native currency and the tenant's main currency for comprehensive analysis and comparison purposes.

      Parameters:
      jdbcTemplate - database template for efficient price data retrieval
      dateFrom - inclusive start date for the statistical analysis period
      dateTo - exclusive end date for the statistical analysis period
      adjustCurrency - flag indicating whether to perform currency conversion
      Returns:
      comprehensive statistical summary with volatility and distribution measures
      Throws:
      IllegalAccessException
      InvocationTargetException
      NoSuchMethodException

    • populateSharpeRatios

      public void populateSharpeRatios(AnnualisedPerformance ap, StatisticsSummary stats, LocalDate dateTo)
      Populates Sharpe ratio fields on each AnnualisedPerformance.AnnualisedYears entry of ap, using the annualised return already on the entry and the annual standard deviation already on stats.

      For each horizon the risk-free leg is the arithmetic mean of RiskFreeRateService.getRateSeries(String, LocalDate, LocalDate) over the window [dateTo.minusYears(horizon), dateTo], falling back to RiskFreeRateService.getRateOnDate(String, LocalDate) at dateTo when the series is empty. The security-currency Sharpe uses the security's currency; the main-currency Sharpe uses the tenant currency.

      Note: every horizon shares a single annualised standard deviation (the one computed by getStandardDeviation(JdbcTemplate, LocalDate, LocalDate, boolean)) — matching the single value already shown to the user in the statistics-summary table on the same page. A per-horizon σ would cost an extra SQL round-trip per horizon and is left as a future refinement.

      The Sharpe value is set to null when the risk-free rate is unmapped for the currency (so the caller can tell missing data from genuine zero), when σ is non-positive (constant series), or when the resulting ratio is non-finite.

      Parameters:
      ap - the annualised performance to extend in place
      stats - the statistics summary containing annualised standard deviation
      dateTo - end of the analysis window (inclusive); each horizon counts back this many years from here
